perturbation.original_text: 1.1 Unless otherwise referenced herein, each of the terms used herein shall have the meaning ascribed to it below: (i) "Affiliate", with respect to any Party hereto, shall mean any legal person, non-legal person economic organization, or natural person, which owns a controlling interest in, or which is controlling, controlled by or under common control with, such Party, directly or indirectly. As used in this Agreement, "control" means the power of any person to direct or cause the direction of management and policies of another party on account of such person's ownership of equity interest, voting right, the right to appoint directors, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 Unless otherwise referenced herein, each of the terms used herein shall have the meaning ascribed to it below: (i) "Affiliate", with respect to any Party hereto, shall mean any entity with a business relationship to Party A or Party B. As used in this Agreement, "control" means the ability of a party to influence the actions of another party.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text modifies the definition of "Affiliate" from entities with a controlling interest to any entity with a business relationship, and redefining control. This introduces an in-text contradiction with later sections relying on the original strict definition of affiliate in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 The Parties shall provide the services hereunder fairly and reasonably as if they were unaffiliated entities in an arm's-length transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Affiliate' from entities with a controlling interest to any entity with a business relationship. The original text defines 'control' as the power to direct management and policies through ownership or contract. The changed text redefines 'control' as merely the ability to influence actions. This change creates a contradiction with section 2.2, which requires the parties to provide services as if they were unaffiliated entities in an arm's-length transaction. Under the original definition, 'unaffiliated' was more clearly defined based on 'control,' whereas the new definition allows for a business relationship to exist while still requiring an arm's-length transaction, introducing ambiguity and potential conflict. If any entity with a mere 'business relationship' is considered an affiliate, it undermines the arm's-length requirement that specifically treats 'unaffiliated entities.'

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE NINE EFFECTIVENESS 9.1 This Agreement shall become effective on the date on which it is signed and affixed with the corporate seals by the authorized representative of each Party and have a term of five (5) years commencing as of the effective date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE NINE EFFECTIVENESS 9.1 This Agreement shall become effective 30 days after the date on which it is signed and affixed with the corporate seals by the authorized representative of each Party and have a term of five (10) years commencing as of the effective date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text contradicts the effectiveness and time the agreement is set for. The original agreement specifies that it's effective immediately, while the new agreement is specified as it's effetive 30 days after the signing of the agreement. As well, the time of the agreement contradicts itself, where original it was for 5 years, while it is now 10 years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 This Agreement shall become effective 30 days after the date on which it is signed and affixed with the corporate seals by the authorized representative of each Party and have a term of five (10) years commencing as of the effective date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters both the effective date and the term length of the agreement. The original text states the agreement is effective immediately upon signing, while the changed text makes it effective 30 days after signing. The original term is five years, while the changed term is ten years. The contradicted text repeats the altered agreement, indicating a failure to resolve the conflicting terms about the agreement's effectiveness and duration, as outlined in the Perturbation Explanation and defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
